action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 5 successfully trained a frozen-head noise detector and used cleanlab to relabel 10,895 training samples (24.21%) into train_cleaned.pt. The detector did not overfit to noise (train_acc 0.59, val_acc 0.55), so the relabeling should be meaningful. Analysis: The previous best clean-test accuracy is 72.67% from a model trained directly on raw noisy labels for 30 epochs. By training on the cleaned labels with additional regularization (drop_path_rate=0.1), we should significantly improve generalization to the clean test set. Decision: Rewrite train.py to be fully robust (support drop_path_rate, cleaned data paths, proper logging), then run a 30-epoch ConvNeXt Tiny fine-tuning on train_cleaned.pt. After training, run a local sanity check with predict.py on val.pt, and immediately submit the best checkpoint for evaluation.
action.command: python train.py --train_pt train_cleaned.pt --val_pt /data/val.pt --model convnext_tiny --epochs 30 --batch_size 128 --lr 1e-3 --weight_decay 0.05 --label_smoothing 0.1 --mixup_alpha 0.2 --warmup_epochs 3 --drop_path_rate 0.1 --checkpoint_dir checkpoints/cleaned && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/cleaned/best_convnext_tiny.pt --output /tmp/local_test.json
